observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the gathering of pertinent documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016 so that authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for write-off of uncollectible receivables totaling P250,000.00 be undertaken upon approval by the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: No records/comments provided in table other than ongoing effort to gather pertinent documents.
observations.reason: On-going effort to gather pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• expedite the reconciliation of the accounts withheld from the employees and remit whatever balance due to the respective National Agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges and enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits from the said offices; and
• provide schedule for the discrepancies noted from the previous years determined as unaccounted balances due to lack of documents to support the remittance and remit it to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: No direct reconciliation schedule submitted. Management noted that reconciliation and preparation of schedule for the discrepancies were still in process and there was no schedule to support the balances.
observations.reason: The reconciliation and preparation of schedule for discrepancies were still in process and there was no schedule to support the balances. Some prior period unreconciled items remain unaccounted due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Property Custodian Designate to prepare and complete the RPCPPE with all the necessary information required and submit to the Audit Team; and
• require the Property Custodian Designate to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of their records, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct balance of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Property Custodian Designate to:
• conduct a physical count of inventories;
• prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories; and
• observe the provisions of Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the prescribed timeliness in the submission of RPCI to allow the necessary verification of inventory account.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the confirmation with the payees/suppliers/contractors and to request them to submit complete documents pertaining to their claims with a period within which to respond shall be indicated in the letter-request for confirmation. In the event they fail to support their claims within such given period, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Property Custodian Designate to prepare the RPCPPE with the prescribed format of the RPCPPE.
• require the Property Custodian Designate to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of their records, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct balance of PPE.
• require the Property Custodian Designate to gather documents that will serve as basis of donated properties and pursue the registration of the remaining motor vehicles as well as those donated properties and not pursue the registration of the four motor vehicles used in daily operations in the name of the Municipality.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor establish an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal of all unserviceable properties and cause its immediate disposal in accordance with applicable rules and regulations in order to avoid further deterioration and possible loss, and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s comments:
The Property Custodian Designate commented that he completed the RPCPPE, however he did not classify the RPCPPE as prescribed by the Manual.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant commented that he opts to consider and to perform the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts through the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Nevertheless, the Property Custodian Designate committed to prepare the RPCPPE as prescribed by the Manual with necessary information thereon, and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the Accounting with the Property records.
observations.reason:
The report lacks necessary information and data such as on hand per count and shortage/overage.
Due To incomplete documents and absence of fair values of the donated vehicles; and due to lack of supporting documents to pursue the registration of the four motor vehicles.
With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in Audit Observation No. 1, Page Nos. 40 to 42, Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2022 dated May 31, 2022 to drop the donated lot from the books of accounts of the Municipality. For item 23, was a road lot not subject for TCT. For item 23, was a road lot not subject for TCT
observations.reason: Gathering and processing of the required documents for Items 1 to 3 are still ongoing for the titling of lots and/or dropping from the books of accounts. No records/documents on file for certain items. Item 24 has no records/ documents on file. Items 25 and 26 still need confirmation; Items 21 to 27 - titling process ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Property Custodian Designate to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories and submit to the Audit Team;
• Municipal Supplies Ledger Accountant to prepare and maintain complete and updated stock cards; and
• Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of inventories initially as an asset account and recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Property Custodian Designate committed to complete the RPCI with necessary information thereon, and she will coordinate with Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of the accounting and property records.
observations.reason: Property Custodian Designate prioritized the preparation of stock cards, thus, the preparation of RPCI was not prepared. Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Cards were updated and maintained by the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian Designate, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the confirmation with the payees/suppliers/contractors and to request them to submit complete documents pertaining to their claims with a period within which to respond shall be indicated in the letter-request for confirmation. In the event they fail to support their claims within such given period, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant commented that the there’s a decentralized on-going assessment per account by department for the Accounts Payable and Guaranty/Security Deposit Payables upon the commencement of the confirmation with the payees/suppliers/contractors and before they revert to the Unappropriated Surplus for unsupported claims.
Nevertheless, the Management committed to comply the recommendations for the confirmation with the payees/suppliers/contractors within the year and proceed to reversion of account upon the failure to support their claims.
observations.reason: Assessment per account by department is on-going due to volume of payables. Reversion procedure pending completion of confirmation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of P232,156.77 to the concerned source agencies pursuant COA Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be used to finance other development projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality appropriated a total amount of P33,000,000.00 for the 20% Development Fund. Per annual accomplishment report, a total of P24,495,234.18 were utilized for the programs, projects and activities which are aligned with the continuous development of the Municipality.
(No direct management action text for this item other than remittal was done.)
observations.reason: The balance of P232,156.77 were remitted to the concerned agencies by the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for payrolls, DTRs, personnel. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted to the Audit Team the Disbursement Vouchers, copies of payrolls, DTRs, copies of personnel certified by the HRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to maximize the utilization of 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed were implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated a total amount of P33,000,000.00 for the 20% Development Fund. Per annual accomplishment report, a total of P24,495,234.18 were utilized for the programs, projects and activities which are aligned with the continuous development of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: 
• prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Donated, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; and 
• prepare the Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
Also, the Municipal Accountant should make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries to reflect the transactions accurately to achieve the fair presentation of the affected account/s.
observations.managementAction: The Summary/List of Donations Received, Issuance of Received, Donated Relief Goods and Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Utilization of Cash Donations was prepared and furnished to the NDRRMC, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009. The Municipal Accountant made necessary correcting/adjusting entries to reflect the transactions accurately to achieve fair presentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team copies of the purchase order together with the supporting documents on the disbursements from BGCM in CY 2020, and regularly furnish the Audit Team with copies of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents within five (5) working days from approval thereof in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to ensure timely review, evaluation and verification thereof.
observations.managementAction: The purchase orders and supporting documents on the disbursements under BGCM in CY 2020 were submitted by the Municipal Accountant on June 11, 2021. Timely submission of perfected contracts was observed by the BAC Secretariat. Copies of perfected contracts were furnished.
observations.reason: Timely submission of perfected contracts was observed by the BAC Secretariat and copies were furnished as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with the payees/suppliers/contractors and to request them to submit complete documents pertaining to their claims. In the event they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: Payables will be requested for reversion as soon as the process of assessing the Accounts Payable and Guaranty/Security Deposit accounts and other necessary procedures were undertaken.
observations.reason: Assessment of payables will take time due to the volume of transactions recorded in the payables account from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts withheld from the employees and the amount remitted to the respective National Agencies and provide the schedule for the discrepancies noted.
observations.managementAction: The balance of remittances will be remitted to the National Treasury due to lack of documents to support the remittance. Remittances totaling P1,756,742.30 and P93,827.40 in January 2021 were made to BIR and Pag-IBIG, respectively, leaving prior period unreconciled/unremitted amounts of P24,589.49 and P12,420.38 for Due to BIR and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts, respectively.
observations.reason: Reconciliation will take time since the balance of the accounts pertains to cumulative transactions from previous years.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Property Custodian Designate to prepare the RPCPPE with the
prescribed format with all the necessary information and coordinate with the
Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of their records, investigate and adjust
any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct balance of
PPE for the fair presentation in the financial statements; and
• require the Property Custodian Designate to gather documents that will
serve as basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded
serviceable properties as well as those donated properties and pursue the
registration of the four motor vehicles used in daily operations in the name of the
Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor establish an Appraisal and Disposal
Committee to expedite the appraisal of all unserviceable properties and cause its
immediate disposal in accordance with applicable rules and regulations in order to
avoid further deterioration and possible loss, and to generate additional income from
its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the person in-charge for
the titling of the parcels of land to:
• request/ask help from other government agencies on how to secure the
titles for Items 1 to 3 of Annex J wherein no available documents can be
found in the Municipality;
• expedite the application of Special Patent and submit all the documents
needed for Items 4 to 13 of Annex J;
• support the claim that the Municipality does not own Item 14 of Annex J
recorded in the books of accounts;
• provide a copy of the documents to the Municipal Accountant for Item 15 to
20 of Annex J as basis for dropping from the books of accounts; and
• expedite the processing of land title for Items 21 to 27 of Annex J.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to drop from the books of accounts the donated lot based on the
Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) No. 066-2018003717 for the fair presentation
of the account in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Property Custodian Designate to prepare the Report on the Physical Count
of Inventories and submit to the Audit Team;
• Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete and updated
Supplies Ledger Cards and the Property Custodian Designate to prepare
and maintain complete and updated stock cards; and
• Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies/inventories
initially as an asset account and recognize the expense account once the
inventories are consumed/distributed in accordance with IPSAS 12 and
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of these accounts in the
financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to expedite the confirmation with the payees/
suppliers/contractors and to request them to submit complete documents
pertaining to their claims with a period within which to respond shall be
indicated in the letter-request for confirmation. In the event they fail to support
their claims within such given period, revert the account to the Unappropriated
Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with the condition that
appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to expedite the gathering of pertinent documents required under COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016 so that authority from the Sangguniang
Bayan through a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for write-off of
uncollectible receivables totaling P250,000.00 is secured/obtained and
eventually, the write-off of these accounts be undertaken upon approval by the
Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the balance of P232,156.77 to the
concerned source agency pursuant COA Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be
used to finance other development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for
evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with
complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
observations.managementAction: PPELC for each category of PPE were updated by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: RPCPPE still lacks necessary information.

observations.recommendation:
• instruct the Designated Property Custodian to prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed format with all the necessary information, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order to establish the correct balance of PPE for the fair presentation in the financial statements; and
• require the Designated Property Custodian to gather documents that will serve as basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded serviceable properties as well as those donated properties and pursue the registration of the four motor vehicles used in daily operations in the name of the Municipality.
Also, the Municipal Mayor to establish an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal of all unserviceable properties and cause its immediate disposal in accordance with applicable rules and regulations in order to avoid further deterioration and possible loss, and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction: Property Cards for each PPE were prepared by the Designated Property Custodian.
observations.reason: No records or documents.

observations.managementAction:
1. Municipal Treasurer to provide a copy of documents related to Title No. T-402631 were provided and were recorded in the books of account by the Municipal Accountant.
2. Municipal Accountant to record the cost directly attributable to the cost.
observations.reason:
Application of Special Patent did not prosper since this was temporarily deferred due to COVID-19 pandemic.
Lack of pertinent documents to support the claim.
No records or documents.

observations.managementAction: Allowance for Impairment for the Accounts Receivables was set up by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Lack of pertinent files/documents to support the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances be fully liquidated at the end of the year for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: All cash advances were fully liquidated during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• MSWD Officer to prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) which shall embody the terms of the project such as: the project name, a brief description of the project, the name of the beneficiary, the date and the amount granted, and the specific period to repay the amounts granted, and to monitor and inspect the project implementation to ensure the success of the project; and
• Municipal Accountant to make an adjustment in the books of accounts by debiting Accounts Payable account and crediting Accounts Receivable account for P20,000.00 to correct the overstatement of these accounts.
observations.managementAction:
MOA was already prepared based on the recommendations.
Necessary adjustments were already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancy and to make the necessary adjustments on the noted discrepancy for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments were already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the P321,105.70, representing the unexpended balance from completed projects of the Municipality, to the concerned source agencies pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be used in other projects or by other agencies needing financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P321,105.70 was already remitted.

Renewal of PARs were already made.
PARs were prepared for the PPE acquired during the year totaling P3,195,786.00.
PARs were renewed and inventory taking were already monitored.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the OIC-Municipal Engineer and implementing units/offices to submit at the beginning of the year the list of infrastructure projects and non-infrastructure PPAs, respectively, that are on-going and those that are to be implemented during the year and providing all the information required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004, to the Acting Municipal Planning and Coordinator (MPDC) for consolidation and submission of the same and the monthly monitoring report to the Audit Team; and
• inform the Audit Team, through the Bids and Awards Committee, within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team was already informed on the infrastructure project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to immediately submit the lacking documents required for the evaluation of the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) by the Commission to secure its approval within the year.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year SWMP was already approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • OIC-Municipal Engineer and implementing units/offices to submit at the beginning of the year the list of infrastructure projects and non-infrastructure PPAs, respectively, that are on-going and those that are to be implemented during the year and providing all the information required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004, to the Acting Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) for consolidation and submission of the same and the monthly monitoring report to the Audit Team; and • inform the Audit Team, through the Bids and Awards Committee, within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Engineer committed to strictly comply with the recommendations. The BAC Chairman committed to strictly comply with the requirement of submitting the report.
observations.reason: The practice of the Engineering Office of submitting the Report on Government PPAs for infrastructure projects to the Accounting Office for validation of their financial aspect cannot be considered as sufficient compliance with requirements under Sections 2.1 and 3.2 of the above-cited COA Circular. Not aware of the guidelines on the submission of notice of award to the Audit Team. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 10, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Accountant to update the PPELC for each category of PPE
and the Designated Property Custodian to prepare the property card for each
PPE and to use the prescribed format of RPCPPE with all the necessary
information, investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted in order
to establish the correct balance of PPE and for the reconciliation of their records
at the end of the year to establish the correct balance of PPE in the financial
statements;
• require the Designated Property Custodian to gather documents that will serve
as basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded serviceable
properties as well as those donated properties and pursue the registration of
the four motor vehicles used in daily operations in the name of the Municipality; and
• instruct the Appraisal Committee to expedite the appraisal of all unserviceable
properties and cause its immediate disposal in accordance with applicable rules
and regulations in order to avoid further deterioration and possible loss, and to
generate additional income from its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
1. person in-charge for the titling of the parcels of land to:
• request/ask help from other government agencies on how to secure the
titles for Items 1 to 3 of Annex I wherein no available documents can be
found in the Municipality;
• secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan for the Municipality’s
application of Special Patent and submit all the documents needed for Items
4 to 12 of Annex I;
• support the claim that the Municipality does not own Item 13 of Annex I
recorded in the books of accounts;
• provide a copy of the documents to the Municipal Accountant for Item 14 of
Annex I as basis for dropping from the books of accounts; and
• expedite the processing of land title for Items 16 to 28 of Annex I.
2. Municipal Treasurer to provide a copy of the documents for the unrecorded land
covered by Title No. T-402631 as basis for recording by the Municipal
Accountant.
3. Municipal Accountant to record the cost directly attributable to the acquisition
of land as part of the purchase price and not as separate land items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• request the Sangguniang Bayan for a Resolution to authorize him to file the
request for write-off of uncollectible receivables totaling P250,000.00 duly
supported by documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 16, 2016; and
• require the Municipal Accountant to set up the provision for Allowance for
Impairment in order to present fairly the receivables account in the financial
statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and for the fair presentation of the accounts in the
financial statements as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period in
accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
Henceforth, ensure that all cash advances be fully liquidated at the end of the year
for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements as required
under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• MSWD Officer to prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) which shall
embody the terms of reference such as: project name, a brief description of the
project, the objectives set forth, the name of the beneficiary, the date and the
amount granted, and the specific period to repay the amounts granted to
beneficiaries, and to monitor and inspect the project implementation to ensure
the success of the project; and
• Municipal Accountant to make an adjustment in the books of accounts by
debiting Accounts Payable account and crediting Accounts Receivable account
for P20,000.00 to correct the overstatement of these accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
confirm with the payees/suppliers/contractors and to request them to submit
complete documents pertaining to their claims. In the event they fail to support
their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund
of the Municipality subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a
Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that
claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
review the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancy and to
make the necessary adjustments on the noted discrepancy of P2,623,992.41 for the
fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
remit the P321,105.70, representing the unexpended balance from completed
projects of the Municipality, to the concerned source agencies pursuant to COA
Circular No. 94-013 so that it can be used in other projects or by other agencies
needing financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated Property
Custodian to:
• allot adequate time to accomplish the renewal of PAR of PPE with over three
years after issuance;
• prepare the PAR and issue to end users for PPE acquired during the year
totaling P3,195,786.00;
• ensure that PAR is renewed every three years or whenever there is a change in
custodianship, in compliance with the existing rules and regulations; and
• facilitate monitoring for inventory taking purposes and to establish full
accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• OIC-Municipal Engineer and implementing units/offices to submit at the
beginning of the year the list of infrastructure projects and non-infrastructure
PPAs, respectively, that are on-going and those that are to be implemented
during the year and providing all the information required under Section 2.1 of
COA Circular No. 2013-004, to the Acting Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) for consolidation and submission of the same and the
monthly monitoring report to the Audit Team; and
• inform the Audit Team, through the Bids and Awards Committee, within ten (10)
days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the
program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices
are already posted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort to complete the documents necessary for the issuance of special patent and for immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor exerted efforts to locate the documents necessary for the issuance of special patent and for the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality; applied for the Special Patent for the Public Market, Municipal Park and School Site in the Community Environment and Natural Resources Office (CENRO) – Pagbilao; Waiting for the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution.
observations.reason: No records or documents. Applications for titling for some parcels of land are in process. Still gathering documents. As regards that part of lot being used by the Municipality but owned by the estate of Atty. Alfredo Pineda, it is recommended that representation may be made. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Designated Property Custodian to continually exert effort to complete the reconciliation of records of the PPE; prepare the RPCPPE with complete information and details using the prescribed format and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE; and require the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.managementAction: • prepare the RPCPPE with complete information and details using the prescribed format and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant • coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE; • requiring the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: Incomplete data in RPCPPE. Information available in the Accounting Office. Implemented the RPCPPE. Due to voluminous property of the Municipality. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 1, Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor continually work for the implementation of the audit recommendations by: coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of PPE records and physical inventory; requiring the Municipal Accountant to obtain the necessary documents and record all the vehicles owned by the Municipality whether serviceable or unserviceable in compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements; requiring the Municipal Treasurer and the Designated Property Custodian to complete the registration of the remaining motor vehicles in the name of the Municipality with absolute ownership in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136; requiring the Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and requiring the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes and for the appraisal of the unserviceable PPE and its disposal.
observations.managementAction: • coordinate with the PPE records and physical inventory were reconciled with remarks as to serviceable or not. • requiring the IIRUP was prepared and forwarded to the Appraisal Committee. • requiring the Designated Property Custodian to prepare IIRUP for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; • requiring the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes and for the appraisal of the unserviceable PPE and its disposal.
observations.reason: Appraisal value not classified as unserviceable yet established. The remaining four motor vehicles are still in process for registering in the name of the Land Transportation Office (LTO). IIRUP was prepared and forwarded to the Appraisal Committee. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 1, Part II of this Report. Not Implemented: No proper turnover of documents when there was a change of composition of personnel in the Accounting Office; Due to retirement of Chairman on the Disposal Committee in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Market Supervisor to: o locate the records that would establish the claims from the 27 beneficiaries; o conduct visits and monitoring of the projects; and o exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned beneficiaries; and • Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the records that would establish the borrowers and the claims, and commence investigation on the recipients of the loan proceeds; and • Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters for payment of the amount collectible.
observations.managementAction: Efforts had been made to locate the documents but to no avail. The Market Supervisor and Municipal Treasurer made inquiries and monitoring but no documents to serve as basis for monitoring were found. The Municipal Accountant noted the recommendation and committed to provide for the allowance for impairment in the 2020 books of accounts. The recommendation to request the Sangguniang Bayan for a Resolution to authorize the Municipal Mayor to file the request for write-off will be done after and in consideration of the ruling of the Office of the Ombudsman since the matter had been forwarded to the said court and now pending decision.
observations.reason: Efforts had been made to locate the documents but to no avail. No turnover of documents. Monitoring was not done due lack of documents. Can no longer be enforced due to absence of documents. Due to passage of time and impossibility of collections. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated Property Custodian to allot adequate time to accomplish the renewal of PAR of PPE with over three years after issuance; prepare the PAR and issue to end users for PPE acquired during the year; ensure that PAR is renewed every three years or whenever there is a change in custodianship, in compliance with the existing rules and regulations; and facilitate monitoring for inventory taking purposes and to establish full accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Designated Property Custodian will conform to the audit recommendations by issuing PAR for PPE’s without PAR and closely monitoring the PAR for renewal every three years or at times whenever necessary.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous PPE owned by the Municipality. Renewal of PARs are ongoing. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 9, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer, send follow-up letters and continue exerting extra effort to secure the approval of its revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the NSWMC pursuant to RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO Designate commented that his office is attending to the corrections and recommendations on the Municipality’s 10-Year SWMP made by the screening committee from the Environmental Management Bureau.
observations.reason: On-going compliance. The Municipality continues to attend to the corrections and recommendations as required by the screening committee. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under observation no. 12, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should continually work for the implementation of the audit recommendations by requiring the: • Market Supervisor to: o locate the records that would establish the claims from the 27 beneficiaries; o conduct visits and monitoring of the projects; and o exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned beneficiaries; • Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the records that would establish the borrowers and the claims, and commence investigation on the recipients of the loan proceeds; and • Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters for payment of the amounts collectible.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Transactions were not vouched and claims cannot be established due to the non-confirmation of receipt by nine out of 27 recipients. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 4, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the
Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort to complete the documents
necessary for the issuance of special patent and for the immediate titling of subject
lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the
future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the Financial
Statements. As regards that part of lot being used by the Municipality but owned by
the estate of Atty. Alfredo Pineda, it is recommended that representation may be
made with the latter for the donation to the Municipality or contract of usufruct.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require theMunicipal Treasurer and
the designated Property Custodian to continually exert effort to complete the
reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the RPCPPE by
undertaking the following:
 prepare the RPCPPE with complete information and details using the prescribed
format and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
 coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the
PPE; and
 also, require the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor continually work for
the implementation of the audit recommendations by:
a) requiring the Municipal Accountant to obtain the necessary documents of all
the vehicles included in the RPCPPE whether acquired thru purchase or non-
exchange transactions, and record all the vehicles owned by the Municipality
whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in
compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements;
b) requiring the Municipal Treasurer and the Designated Property Custodian to
complete the registration of the remaining motor vehicles in the name of the
Municipality with absolute ownership in accordance with Section 7 of RA No.
4136;
c) requiring the Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and IIRUP for
the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in
accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and
d) requiring the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the
vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording
purposes and for the appraisal of the unserviceable PPE and its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should continually work for the
implementation of the audit recommendations by requiring the:
 Market Supervisor to:
  o locate the records that would establish the claims from the 27
    beneficiaries;
  o conduct visits and monitoring of the projects; and
  o exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned
    beneficiaries;
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the
  records that would establish the borrowers and the claims, and commence
  investigation on the recipients of the loan proceeds; and
 Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters for payment of the amounts
  collectible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property Custodian
Designate to ensure that Property Acknowledgement Receipt is renewed every
three years or whenever there is a change in custodianship, in compliance with
Section 492 of the Government Auditing and Accounting Manual (GAAM), Volume
I, in order that record of accountability for PPEs is updated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit at the beginning of the year the list
  of all on-going PPA and those that are to be implemented for the year
  providing the information as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No.
  2013-004;
 inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure
  project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
  signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
 submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the existing
  regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort to complete the documents necessary for the issuance of special patent and for the immediate titling of subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386, and for the fair presentation of the Land account in the Financial Statements. As regards that part of lot being used by the Municipality but owned by the estate of Atty. Alfredo Pineda, it is recommended that representation may be made with the latter for the donation to the Municipality or contract of usufruct.
observations.managementAction: A letter issued by the President of the Lucban Historical Society certifies that the parcels of land where the Municipal Building, Municipal Park, and Public Market are situated since time memorial. Cadastral maps and its technical descriptions are still acquired as it is necessary for the titling of the parcels of land.
observations.reason: The documents required for titling and special patent issuance are still incomplete. With the new developments, the Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the designated Property Custodian and Supply Officer to continually exert effort to complete the reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the RPCPPE by undertaking the following: • prepare the RPCPPE with complete information and details using the prescribed format and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; • coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE; and • also, require the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.managementAction: A copy of RPCPPE was submitted to the Municipal Accountant. The Property Custodian Designate to coordinate with the new full-fledged Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE. The Municipal Accountant started to update the PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: Large volume of PPE started to be reconciled. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor continually work for the implementation of the audit recommendations by: a) requiring the Municipal Accountant to obtain the necessary documents of all the vehicles included in the RPCPPE whether acquired thru purchase or non-exchange transactions, and record all the vehicles owned by the Municipality whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate asset accounts in compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the financial statements; b) requiring the Municipal Treasurer and the Designated Property Custodian to complete the registration of the remaining motor vehicles in the name of the Municipality with absolute ownership in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136; c) requiring the Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and IIRUP for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and d) requiring the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes and for the appraisal of the unserviceable PPE and its disposal.
observations.managementAction: Designated Property Custodian informed that he already prepared the IIRUP and he will update it after the reconciliation should they discover other unserviceable PPE. The Management also committed to train the Appraisal Committee through seminars offered by COA for them to gain knowledge on the appraisal and disposal process.
observations.reason: Some vehicles are still for registration. There are acquired vehicles still for registration. No appraisal was made yet due to lack of knowledge in the appraisal process. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to refrain/to stop the practice of granting elected cash advances to officials and employees who are not duly appointed or designated disbursing officers and not bonded, pursuant to Section 101(2) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002. We further recommend that the OIC - Municipal Accountant limit the cash advance of elected officials to their travelling expenses only.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the justification/explanation on the donations made to private associations and the use of government funds for private purposes was refrained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • submit Justification letter/justification/explanation on the donations made to private associations; and • refrain from using government funds for private purposes and adhere with the mandates of the Office of the Sanggunian and Section 335 of RA No. 7160 and Section 4 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor submitted justification/explanation. Use of government funds for private purposes was refrained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • submit the Liquidation Report to the ICFAU. Furnish a copy of the duly received transmittal letter to the Audit Team upon receipt; and • comply with the existing guidelines on confidential and intelligence fund.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation Report was submitted to ICFAU and received. Guidelines were strictly complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from appropriating funds for projects for execution by the Office of the SB, except for projects for the Office of the SB, such as construction of building which may be provided under the said Office.
observations.managementAction: The budget was augmented to remove the appropriations allotted for projects for the Office of the Sanggunian except for projects for the Office of the SB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Designated MENRO, secure the approval of its revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and effect the provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the controlled dumpsites and continue to develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO sent their first official follow-up to the National Solid Waste Management Commission on June 17, 2019. Copy was furnished to the audit team. The Management committed to continually comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Controlled Dumpsites are already phased-out and sanitary landfills are used as final disposal site. However, the revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan is still not approved yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal DRRMO to obtain a sufficient understanding of his assignment, its nature and functions, as well as the reportorial requirement such as but not limited to submitting to the Audit Team regularly the annual report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor submitted to the Audit Team the report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point person to obtain a sufficient understanding of her assignment, its nature and functions, as well as the reportorial requirement such as but not limited to the submission of the Municipality’s GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adherence to the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: GAD Accomplishment Report was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.reason: Cash Advances were still granted to officials and employees who are not duly appointed or designated disbursing officers and not bonded. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 4, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to
request from COA for the write-off of receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: The claims could not be established due to absence of any proof of receipt of loan proceeds from the beneficiaries. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to submit
to the Auditor all the required
supporting documents for
evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Various lacking documents are still for submission. Notice of Suspension was already issued.
observations.reason: Various lacking documents are still for submission. Notice of Suspension was already issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal person to regularly submit the Municipality’s GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and strictly adhere to the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to strictly comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Due to inaction, the Municipality’s GAD Accomplishment Report was not submitted. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 12, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Due to lack of knowledge in this requirement, the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund was not submitted. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 11, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor
and the Municipal Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort to complete the
documents necessary for the issuance of special patents for the immediate titling of
subject lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may
arise in the future and in compliance with Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section
148 of COA Circular No. 92-386, and for the fair presentation of the Land account
in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer
and the designated Property Custodian and Supply Officer to continually exert effort
to complete the reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the
RCPPE by undertaking the following:
 prepare the RPCPPE with complete information and details using the
prescribed format and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
 coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records
of the PPE; and
 also, require the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to continually work for the
implementation of the audit recommendations by requiring the:
 a) Municipal Accountant to validate the ownership and determine the mode
of acquisition whether thru purchase or non-exchange transactions of all
the vehicles included in the RPCPPE and record all the vehicles owned by
the Municipality whether serviceable or unserviceable to the appropriate
asset accounts in compliance with PPSAS 17 for proper presentation in the
financial statements;
 b) Appraisal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine
the fair values at acquisition dates for recording purposes;
 c) Municipal Treasurer and the Designated Property Custodian to complete
the registrations of the remaining motor vehicles in the name of the
Municipality with absolute ownership in accordance with Section 7 of RA
No. 4136; and
 d) Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the disposal of
unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with
Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to refrain/to stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials and
employees who are not duly appointed or designated disbursing officers and not
bonded, pursuant to Section 101(2) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002. We
further recommend that the OIC- Municipal Accountant limit the cash advance of
elected officials to their travelling expenses only.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
 Market Supervisor and the Municipal Agriculturist to:
o locate the records that would establish the claims from the 27
beneficiaries;
o conduct visitorial and monitoring of the projects; and
o exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned
beneficiaries;
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to
locate the records that would establish the borrowers and the claims; and
 Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters for payment of the amounts
collectible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 submit justification/explanation on the donations made to private
associations; and
 refrain from using government funds for private purposes and adhere with
the mandates of Section 335 of RA No. 7160 and Section 4 of PD No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
 submit the Liquidation Report to the ICFAU and furnish a copy of the
duly received transmittal letter to the Audit Team; and
 strictly comply with the existing guidelines on confidential and
intelligence fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to refrain from appropriating funds for projects for execution of budgets
or implementation by the Office of the Sanggunian except for projects for the
Office of the SB, such as construction of building which may be provided under the
said Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for
evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Designated MENRO,
secure the approval of its revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the
National Solid Waste Management Commission and effect the provisions in their
plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the controlled dump sites and
continue to develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site
pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Officer to obtain a sufficient understanding of his
assignment, its nature and functions, as well as the reportorial requirement such as
but not limited to submitting to the Audit Team regularly the annual report on
Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD focal point
person to obtain a sufficient understanding of her assignment, its nature and
functions, as well as the reportorial requirement such as but not limited to the
submission of the Municipality’s GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed and
strictly adherence to the existing regulations.

We reiterated that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor and the Municipal
Treasurer to make an extra effort
to facilitate the immediate titling
of subject lots in the name of the
Municipality to avoid contingent
claims that may arise in the future
and in compliance with Section
39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section
148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: A query with the Land Management Bureau - DENR regarding the requirements for the processing of issuance of Special Patents was made and the steps regarding the application for the issuance of Special Patent for various lots were already completed.
observations.reason: Due to incomplete document necessary for the special patents issuance. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
 Municipal Treasurer
and the designated Property
Custodian and Supply Officer
to:
 prepare the RPCPPE
with complete information and details using the
prescribed format and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
 coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records of the PPE; and
 also, require the Municipal Accountant to update the PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.managementAction: The RPCPPE was prepared. Updating the PPE Ledger Cards is on process.
observations.reason: Due to non completion of reconciliation because of incomplete details of some PPE. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our
recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Accountant to
validate the ownership and
determine the mode of
acquisition whether through
purchase or non-exchange
transactions of all the
vehicles included in the
RPCPPE and record all the
vehicles owned by the
Municipality whether
serviceable or unserviceable
to the appropriate asset
accounts in compliance with
PPSAS 17 for proper
presentation in the financial
statements;
b) Appraisal Committee to
expedite the appraisal of the
vehicles to determine the fair
values at acquisition dates for
recording purposes;
c) Municipal Treasurer and
Designated Property Custodian to register the Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality with absolute ownership in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136; and
d) Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Some vehicles were already recorded in the books of accounts. The Designated Property Custodian furnished the Audit Team of the updated registration of the motor vehicles. He also informed the Audit Team that some documents were not turned over to him, making it difficult to facilitate the registration of the other remaining motor vehicles. He also informed that he already prepared the IIRUP and he will update it after the reconciliation should they discover other unserviceable PPE. It was informed that the Municipality has no active Appraisal Committee to facilitate the appraisal of the unserviceable PPE and cause its disposal.
observations.reason: Some vehicles are still for validation. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Engineer to submit the:
 list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2016; and
 monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The list of all on-going government projects/ programs/ activities and monitoring was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the
MPDC to coordinate with the
DPWH and make follow-ups if
necessary regarding the status of
the remaining NCA for P5,000,000.00 under SARO Nos. A-13-00415 and A-13000001805.
observations.managementAction: The remaining NCA was already released.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the
members of the Local School
Board to:
a. prepare a balanced budget with conscious and prioritization of expenses;
b. refrain from accommodating Activities mandated by DepEd for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;
c. request for input from the DepEd officials or Schools Division Superintendent on the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations; and
d. maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the projects/programs and activities (PPAs), and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Balanced budget and prioritization of expenses were observed in utilizing the Special Education Fund. Activities mandated by DepEd were not accommodated. SEF projects and expenditures observed in accordance with the DepEd criteria. SEF utilization was maximized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the NSWMC and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Additions to Chapters in the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality were submitted.
observations.reason: The Municipality’s revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was submitted and is still for review and approval. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 10, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
Municipal Mayor:
 require the MPDC to ensure that the mandatory Development Fund appropriated out of the IRA Fund be fully utilized to implement prioritized identified programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable outcome on socio-economic development and environmental Management outlined in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1.
 install regular monitoring mechanism to assess the progress of project implementation so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
 ensure that a closer coordination among the Offices officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring is enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency.
observations.managementAction: The programs/ Fund 20% Development Fund were implemented. Progress of project implementation is monitored. Coordination between Offices is enhanced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Notes to FS were prepared and submitted to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements which will guide the users/readers in making sound financial decision.
observations.managementAction: Notes to Financial Statements were submitted.
