Appraisal Committee to expedite the appraisal of the vehicles to determine the fair valuesat acquisition dates for recording purposes;
Municipal Treasurer and Designated Property Custodian to register the Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality with absolute ownership in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136; and
Designated Property Custodian to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances to officials and employees who are not duly appointed or designated disbursing officers and not bonded, in compliance with Section 101(2) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to request fromCOA for the write-off of receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit the:
list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year, as of January 1, 2016; and
monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to coordinate with the DPWH and make follow-ups if necessary regarding the status of the remaining NCA for P5,000,000.00 under SARO Nos. A-13-00415 and A-13000001805.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor all the required supporting documents for evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year SWMP from the NSWMC and make provisions in their plan for the closure or eventual phase out of the controlled dump sites within the period specified under the framework and develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions under the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the MPDC to ensure that the mandatory Development Fund appropriated out of the IRA be fully utilized to implement prioritized identified programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable outcome on socio-economic development and environmental Management outlined in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1.
install regular monitoring mechanism to assess the progress of project implementation so that all its programmed development  projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
ensure that a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring is enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the Notes to FS were prepared and submitted to enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements which will guide the users/readers in making sound financial decision.

We reiterated that the Municipal    Mayor instruct the Municipal        Treasurer to maintain property cards and the Inventory Committee to      complete the physical inventory of  all property of the Municipality and   prepare and submit immediately the  RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: Physical count of all property of the Municipality was made and submitted to this Office.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Treasurer to register the Motor Vehicles in the name of the Municipality in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 4136 and COA Circular No. 77-63 dated January 24, 1977.
observations.managementAction: Registration of the Motor Vehicles is  currently ongoing.
observations.reason: Volume of properties for registration. Reiterated in Part II, Observation No.3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor: designate a responsible person to   facilitate titling of subject lots  in the name of the Municipality to  avoid contingent claims that may    arise in the future and in          compliance with Section 148 of COA  Circular No. 92-386;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor, Mr. Cristofin J. Nantes was designated to be in charge of the titling of lots.

observations.recommendation: create an Appraisal Committee to    conduct the appraisal of the 22 lots for recording purposes;
observations.managementAction: The appraisal committee was established.

observations.recommendation: require the Municipal Accountant to    record all the lots owned by the Municipality; and
observations.managementAction: Some of the lots were recorded.
observations.reason: Other lots were still for verification.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal    Mayor require the Municipal         Treasurer to ensure that the COs    remit promptly and intact their     collections up to the last official    receipt issued, in compliance with    the above cited provision of the    MNGAS to avoid loss and/or misuse of  funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer ensures that Collecting Officers promptly remit their collections.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation    that the Municipal Mayor require    the: Market Supervisor and Municipal  Agriculturist to: locate the records that would establish the borrowers and the claims; conduct visitorial and monitoring of the projects; and exert all efforts to collect the said receivables from the concerned beneficiaries;
observations.managementAction: Efforts in locating the records were made.
observations.reason: No records were found that will establish the borrowers and their claims.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant and the        Municipal Treasurer to exert efforts to locate the records that would establish the borrowers and the claims;
observations.managementAction: Efforts in locating the records were made.
observations.reason: No records were found that will establish the borrowers and their claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor require the: concerned accountable officers to settle/liquidate immediately their cash advances. Failure to do so, implement the withholding of their salaries or any amount due them, to be applied to their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances were liquidated in January 2016.

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the regulations on proper granting of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and see to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Regulations on proper granting and liquidation of cash advances were observed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation    that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert the above-enumerated income recorded in the Trust Fund to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Income recorded in the Trust Fund was transferred to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal    Mayor require the MPDC to coordinate with DPWH and make follow-ups, if, necessary, regarding the status of the remaining NCA under SARO Nos. A-13-00415 and A-13000001805.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up letters were sent to the DPWH.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor require the Municipal         Accountant to record the vehicle    received as donation in the         appropriate accounts at fair value  in compliance with Section 112 of     P.D. No. 1445, PPSAS 17 and PPSAS 23 for proper presentation in the   financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The vehicle was recorded under JEV No. 100-1605-054 dated May 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor require the Local Development  Council, the MPDC Office and the      Budget Office to comply with the     guidelines on the appropriation and   utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in order to provide the constituents of the Municipality the immediate benefits that may be derived from the developmental projects and programs prescribed in the said Joint Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: Provision of P116,399.98 in the General Fund budget was made under Resolution No. 234-2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to advise the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjusting entries to ensure fair presentation of affected accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting Entry was made under JEV no. 300-1605-007 dated June 30, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor adhere strictly with the     provisions of Local Budget Memorandum No. 28 to best serve the ultimate goal of the GAD programs, such as gender equality and women empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Budget was made in compliance with the Local Budget Memorandum No. 28.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal   Mayor require the Municipal         Engineer, BAC Chairperson and the   Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor for evaluation all the required documents.
observations.managementAction: Lacking documents were submitted on September 22, 2016 and October 10, 2016, respectively.
